rain streaming down her face, eyes wide open, unblinking
head tilting at an unnaturally slow birdlike angle
mid-exhale of cigarette smoke she doesn't actually need
pushing wet hair back, fangs visible in slight grimace
nostrils flaring sharply as she catches an unknown scent
breath misting cold air, her own exhale never appears
eyes half-lidded in boredom spanning entire human lifetimes
completely motionless mid-thought, frozen like a wax figure
running one pale finger slowly along her own jaw
eyes tracking something moving just past the camera's edge
snowflakes melting instantly on her corpse-cold exposed skin
chin resting on knuckles, faint smirk at ancient joke
wind catching hair across one eye, fangs barely visible
staring at something behind camera without single blink
slight nostril-flare of contempt at mortal conversation nearby
touching the hollow of her own throat absently
pupils shifting focus to something moving behind you
frost forming on eyelashes, skin unnaturally pristine beneath
half-smile remembering something from three centuries past
cracking her neck with eerie deliberate slowness
fog curling around her jaw and collarbones like smoke
head cocked like predator hearing prey two rooms away
drumming one fingernail on her own collarbone, distracted
pressing her own temple as if recalling painful memory
jaw clenched in ancient restraint, fangs pressing lower lip
